purpose: Build two complementary operator surfaces for Ananta: a Vim/Neovim plugin for developer-centric coding workflows and a terminal UI for admin, task, artifact, approval, audit and KRITIS-style operations.
goal: Developers should be able to use Ananta directly from Vim/Neovim for coding and review workflows, while operators and advanced users can manage goals, tasks, artifacts, approvals, logs, repair flows and KRITIS-related visibility through a dedicated Ananta TUI.
program_success_criteria: The Vim/Neovim plugin acts as an editor-native adapter to Ananta and does not duplicate orchestration logic.
The Ananta TUI provides a practical terminal-native control surface for admin and operational workflows.
Both surfaces use the same backend contracts, approval model, audit model and task/artifact logic.
The plugin and TUI together improve usability without fragmenting the platform into inconsistent interfaces.

groups.title: Gemeinsame Grundlage fuer Plugin und TUI
groups.goal: Create shared frontend contracts, API assumptions, view-model semantics and UX boundaries for both terminal-facing surfaces.

tasks.title: Shared UI boundary model define
acceptance_criteria: A design note defines what belongs in the Vim/Neovim plugin, what belongs in the TUI and what remains browser-first.
The model states that orchestration, routing, governance and approvals remain in Ananta.
The split between developer-facing and operator-facing views is explicit.

tasks.title: Shared frontend contract inventory create
acceptance_criteria: A contract inventory maps the required backend endpoints and read models for plugin and TUI usage.
The inventory covers auth, goals, tasks, artifacts, approvals, logs, health, runtime diagnostics and relevant KRITIS views.
The inventory identifies missing API surfaces before UI implementation starts.

tasks.title: Common object model for task artifact approval log entries define
acceptance_criteria: A normalized object model is defined for task, artifact, approval, log and trace items used by both UIs.
The object model is stable enough to avoid each frontend inventing different interpretations.
The model supports compact rendering and detailed drill-down.

tasks.title: Connection profile abstraction create
acceptance_criteria: Both the plugin and the TUI can use a consistent connection profile concept for endpoint, auth and environment selection.
Connection profiles can distinguish local, team and KRITIS-oriented targets.
The design supports future multiple Ananta instances.

tasks.title: Shared permission and capability surface define
acceptance_criteria: Both UIs can understand capability availability and permission limits in a consistent way.
Sensitive operations such as approvals, writes, repair actions, terminal usage and admin actions are clearly modeled.
The design aligns with existing platform governance and auth semantics.

tasks.title: Common error and degraded-state model define
acceptance_criteria: Both UIs distinguish auth failure, backend unreachability, missing capability, policy denial and runtime failure cleanly.
Error semantics remain understandable to real users.
The design avoids silent fallbacks.

tasks.title: Shared trace and audit linking rules define
acceptance_criteria: Plugin and TUI both know how to link tasks, approvals, artifacts, logs and audit traces.
The linking model is consistent across both surfaces.
The model supports future KRITIS evidence visibility.

tasks.title: First-run and onboarding split define
acceptance_criteria: A clear onboarding split exists: plugin for coding workflows, TUI for operations/admin flows.
Users are not forced into the wrong interface for the wrong job.
The documentation can explain the choice quickly.

tasks.title: Shared release and smoke strategy define
acceptance_criteria: There is a shared smoke and release approach for plugin and TUI.
The approach covers both local and containerized Ananta targets.
Core flows are validated before claiming usability.

groups.title: Vim/Neovim Plugin Grundlage
groups.goal: Create a real editor-native integration for coding, review, context and goal submission.

tasks.title: Plugin architecture decide
acceptance_criteria: A concrete architecture choice is made for Vim and/or Neovim support.
The decision covers whether to support Neovim first and Vim later or both from the beginning.
The architecture is maintainable and not overengineered.

tasks.title: Minimum supported editor matrix define
acceptance_criteria: Supported Neovim/Vim versions are documented.
Plugin dependencies and optional runtime requirements are listed.
The first release path is practical for installation.

tasks.title: Connection and authentication support implement
acceptance_criteria: The plugin can store and use Ananta connection profiles safely.
Authentication works with the supported Ananta auth path.
Secrets are not leaked into command history or logs.

tasks.title: Plugin command surface define
acceptance_criteria: A coherent command set exists for the plugin.
The first command set covers goal submission, analyze, review, patch, new project and evolve project.
Command naming is editor-appropriate and aligned with Ananta concepts.

tasks.title: Editor context collection implement
acceptance_criteria: The plugin can collect current file, buffer, cursor context, selection and project root.
Context collection stays bounded and visible to the user.
The plugin avoids dumping excessive unrelated workspace content.

tasks.title: Goal submission from editor implement
acceptance_criteria: Users can send a goal directly from the editor.
Goal submission can include current file or selected text context.
The flow favors official Ananta use cases rather than arbitrary hidden behavior.

tasks.title: Quick action palette or command wrappers add
acceptance_criteria: Common operations can be invoked quickly from editor commands or a lightweight picker.
The first useful operations are obvious and easy to trigger.
The plugin does not require users to memorize too much before becoming useful.

groups.title: Vim/Neovim Plugin - Coding Review Kontext Goals
groups.goal: Deliver the core developer-facing workflows inside the editor.

tasks.title: Analyze current file or project flow add
acceptance_criteria: The plugin supports asking Ananta to analyze the current file or project context.
Results are shown in a readable editor-native way.
The flow can be triggered without leaving the editor.

tasks.title: Review selected code flow add
acceptance_criteria: The plugin supports sending a visual selection or current block for review.
Review output is linked back to the relevant code area.
The review flow supports safe, bounded context submission.

tasks.title: Patch planning and fix suggestion flow add
acceptance_criteria: The plugin can request patch planning or fix suggestions for the current context.
Results do not silently mutate files.
Suggestions are presented with clear user control.

tasks.title: Task list and current task context view add
acceptance_criteria: The plugin can show current Ananta tasks relevant to the current project or user.
The user can inspect task summaries and status without leaving the editor.
Task navigation is editor-friendly and compact.

tasks.title: Artifact preview inside editor add
acceptance_criteria: Relevant artifacts can be previewed in a scratch buffer, split or floating window.
The artifact rendering is readable for text-heavy outputs such as summaries and review results.
Unsupported artifact types degrade gracefully.

tasks.title: Context inspection panel add
acceptance_criteria: The user can inspect what context will be sent to Ananta before submission.
The panel shows file path, selection size and included project information.
The user can trim or cancel context submission.

tasks.title: Diff and proposal rendering add
acceptance_criteria: Review proposals or diff-like outputs can be rendered in a Vim-friendly form.
The user can inspect changes before any application step.
The plugin preserves a review-first posture.

tasks.title: Navigation between tasks artifacts and source locations add
acceptance_criteria: The plugin can jump from task or artifact items back to the relevant file or location where possible.
Cross-navigation feels editor-native instead of web-like.
The navigation model remains predictable.

tasks.title: Open in browser or open in Ananta shortcuts add
acceptance_criteria: The plugin can open deeper task, artifact or goal views in the browser when editor rendering is insufficient.
The shortcut is available but not forced.
The plugin stays thin where browser views are better.

groups.title: Vim/Neovim Plugin - Erweiterte Features
groups.goal: Add richer features without turning the plugin into a second orchestration system or IDE clone.

tasks.title: Blueprint-aware project start commands add
acceptance_criteria: The plugin can surface standard blueprint or work-profile choices for new or evolving project flows.
The user can start official product paths from the editor.
The plugin reflects product terminology consistently.

tasks.title: Approval awareness for risky actions add
acceptance_criteria: The plugin can show when an action is pending approval or blocked by policy.
The UI does not imply that writes will happen immediately.
Approval state remains explicit.

tasks.title: Trace and diagnostic summary view add
acceptance_criteria: Advanced users can inspect compact trace or routing summaries.
The view helps debugging without overwhelming ordinary use.
The feature remains optional.

tasks.title: Knowledge and context source summary add
acceptance_criteria: The plugin can show from which source classes context came when relevant.
The summary is compact and understandable.
The feature supports trust in responses.

tasks.title: First-run guided setup inside editor add
acceptance_criteria: A first-run flow helps users connect the plugin and perform one useful operation quickly.
The flow recommends an official use case such as analyze or review.
The setup does not demand browser use for the first success.

groups.title: Ananta TUI Grundlage
groups.goal: Build a dedicated terminal UI for operational use without trying to become a full editor.

tasks.title: TUI framework and architecture decide
acceptance_criteria: A concrete TUI stack is selected.
The decision fits long-lived admin and dashboard-like workflows.
The architecture supports multiple views without becoming brittle.

tasks.title: TUI information architecture define
acceptance_criteria: The TUI information architecture is defined clearly.
The design covers dashboard-like overview, drill-down views and action flows.
The TUI remains operation-focused and does not attempt to replace the browser for everything.

tasks.title: Authentication and session handling implement
acceptance_criteria: The TUI can authenticate securely against Ananta.
Session handling is safe for long-running terminal use.
Sensitive credentials are handled carefully and not echoed.

tasks.title: Global navigation and layout implement
acceptance_criteria: The TUI has a coherent layout and navigation model.
Users can move between key views without getting lost.
The layout works in realistic terminal sizes.

tasks.title: Connection and runtime status header add
acceptance_criteria: The TUI shows current connection profile, target instance and basic health signals.
The header makes the current context obvious.
The status presentation is compact and useful.

groups.title: Ananta TUI - Admin Tasks Artifacts
groups.goal: Provide the core terminal-native operational workflows.

tasks.title: Task board or task list view implement
acceptance_criteria: The TUI has a practical task list or board view.
Users can inspect status, priority, review-needed state and recent progress.
The view supports real operational use.

tasks.title: Task detail view implement
acceptance_criteria: A selected task can be inspected in detail.
The detail view includes summary, stage/routing hints, artifacts and latest state.
The view stays readable in terminal form.

tasks.title: Artifact list and artifact detail view implement
acceptance_criteria: The TUI can list artifacts and open artifact details.
Text-heavy artifacts render clearly in terminal format.
Users can move between artifacts and related tasks.

tasks.title: Goal list and goal detail view add
acceptance_criteria: Goals can be listed and inspected in the TUI.
The operator can see how goals relate to tasks and artifacts.
The view is useful for current operational monitoring.

tasks.title: Goal submission and quick action entry add
acceptance_criteria: Users can submit a goal or launch a few key actions from the TUI.
The TUI supports the most important official use cases.
The flow remains controlled and explicit.

tasks.title: Task filtering and grouping add
acceptance_criteria: Tasks can be filtered by status, review-required, blocked or similar useful classes.
Grouping remains practical and not overly complex.
The filtering model improves daily use.

groups.title: Ananta TUI - Logs Approvals Audit
groups.goal: Make the TUI the strong terminal surface for high-signal operational visibility.

tasks.title: Log stream and log detail views implement
acceptance_criteria: The TUI can display relevant logs in an operator-friendly way.
Users can inspect current and recent logs without leaving the terminal.
The log view stays readable and filterable.

tasks.title: Approval queue view implement
acceptance_criteria: Pending approvals can be listed in a dedicated TUI view.
The view shows enough context to make safe decisions.
The queue supports real approval workflows.

tasks.title: Approval detail and action flow add
acceptance_criteria: A selected approval item can be reviewed and explicitly approved or rejected when policy allows.
The action flow is safe, explicit and auditable.
The TUI does not encourage blind approval.

tasks.title: Audit summary view implement
acceptance_criteria: The TUI can display compact audit summaries suitable for operators.
The view can highlight sensitive actions, blocked actions and recent critical chains.
The view stays high-signal and not overwhelming.

tasks.title: Audit trace drill-down add
acceptance_criteria: Operators can inspect more detailed trace-linked audit information from the TUI.
The drill-down supports incident and approval review work.
The feature respects redaction and RBAC.

tasks.title: Policy denial and blocked action view add
acceptance_criteria: The TUI can surface blocked actions and policy denials in a dedicated or filtered view.
Operators can tell why an action was denied.
The feature is helpful for debugging and governance review.

groups.title: Ananta TUI - KRITIS Repair Admin
groups.goal: Make the TUI the preferred terminal surface for hardened operational and repair-oriented flows.

tasks.title: KRITIS dashboard summary view add
acceptance_criteria: A KRITIS-oriented overview exists for audit health, approval backlog, mutation status and policy posture.
The view helps operators quickly understand system trust posture.
The summary is compact enough for real terminal use.

tasks.title: Repair session list and detail views add
acceptance_criteria: If system repair flows exist, the TUI can inspect repair sessions and their state.
The operator can review diagnosis, plan, applied steps and verification results.
The view is useful for local admin scenarios.

tasks.title: Repair approval and execution review flow add
acceptance_criteria: The TUI can support bounded approval and review for repair-related steps when policy allows.
The flow is explicit and auditable.
The TUI supports dry-run-first usage where applicable.

tasks.title: Health and runtime diagnostics view add
acceptance_criteria: The TUI shows health, readiness and important runtime diagnostics for the current instance.
The view is good enough for daily operational checking.
The information is well structured.

tasks.title: Provider and backend visibility view add
acceptance_criteria: Operators can inspect current providers, backends and important capability states.
The view supports runtime troubleshooting and policy review.
The view is operator-focused and not too noisy.

groups.title: Ananta TUI - Navigation Bedienbarkeit Robustheit
groups.goal: Make the TUI genuinely usable over time rather than only technically possible.

tasks.title: Keyboard navigation and shortcuts refine
acceptance_criteria: Important actions are available through keyboard-friendly navigation.
The TUI feels natural for terminal-heavy users.
The shortcut model is learnable.

tasks.title: Search and filtering across key views add
acceptance_criteria: Users can search or filter tasks, artifacts, approvals and logs where appropriate.
The filtering behavior remains consistent across views.
The feature materially improves navigation.

tasks.title: State persistence and resume behavior add
acceptance_criteria: The TUI can restore useful session state such as selected profile or last active view.
Sensitive data is not persisted unsafely.
Resume behavior reduces friction.

tasks.title: Empty and error state UX add
acceptance_criteria: Each major TUI view has practical empty and error states.
The user can distinguish no data, permission denial and backend failure.
The interface stays understandable under degraded conditions.

groups.title: Integration Dokumentation Tests Releases
groups.goal: Harden both surfaces with docs, tests and release discipline.

tasks.title: Plugin automated tests add
acceptance_criteria: Core plugin flows are protected by automated tests.
Tests cover connection handling, context collection and command result parsing.
The suite is practical for ongoing maintenance.

tasks.title: TUI automated tests add
acceptance_criteria: Core TUI view models and interaction flows have automated coverage.
The suite covers task, artifact, log and approval handling at minimum.
The suite protects against regressions as the TUI grows.

tasks.title: Plugin manual smoke checklist add
acceptance_criteria: A short manual smoke checklist exists for the Vim/Neovim plugin.
The checklist covers connect, analyze, review and task/artifact viewing.
The checklist is usable before releases.

tasks.title: TUI manual smoke checklist add
acceptance_criteria: A short manual smoke checklist exists for the TUI.
The checklist covers task view, artifact view, log view and approval flow.
The checklist is suitable for terminal-centric release validation.

tasks.title: Plugin user guide write
acceptance_criteria: A practical user guide exists for the Vim/Neovim plugin.
The guide explains installation, setup and first useful workflows.
The guide matches the actual shipped command set.

tasks.title: TUI user and operator guide write
acceptance_criteria: A guide exists for using the TUI in admin and operational contexts.
The guide explains navigation, core views and common operator tasks.
The guide includes safety notes for approval and repair flows.

tasks.title: Common docs explaining when to use plugin versus TUI write
acceptance_criteria: Documentation clearly explains which interface is intended for which class of work.
Users can choose the right interface quickly.
The split between coding and operations is obvious.

tasks.title: Release packaging and versioning strategy define
acceptance_criteria: A packaging and versioning strategy exists for the plugin and the TUI.
Compatibility with Ananta backend versions is documented.
The strategy supports maintainable releases.

tasks.title: Golden path demos for plugin and TUI add
acceptance_criteria: There is one official golden path for the Vim/Neovim plugin.
There is one official golden path for the TUI.
Both demos are reproducible by another developer.

execution_order_summary: Then NVIM-PLUGIN-FOUNDATION and TUI-FOUNDATION in parallel
